ai_analysis.summary: Clients are returning primarily due to ongoing structural disadvantages that create recurring financial crises, including relationship breakdowns, job loss, health issues, and housing instability. Many are single mothers, migrants with visa uncertainties, or individuals facing multiple intersecting hardships that prevent long-term financial stability.

themes.label: Relationship breakdown and family violence
themes.description: Separation, divorce, or escaping domestic violence creates new financial pressures, particularly for mothers now supporting children alone. These clients face immediate housing crises and loss of spousal financial support.

themes.label: Employment loss and income instability
themes.description: Job loss or reduced work capacity due to health issues creates recurring financial emergencies. Clients struggle with debt accumulation and basic living expenses while seeking new employment.

themes.label: Migration and visa-related vulnerabilities
themes.description: Clients with uncertain visa status, recent arrivals, or those paying migration lawyer fees face ongoing financial instability. Limited access to government support compounds their financial difficulties.

themes.label: Health crises and medical expenses
themes.description: Serious health conditions like cancer or diabetes create ongoing financial strain through medical costs and reduced work capacity. These clients face recurring expenses for treatment and specialist care.
